assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map's 'Ambiguities Identified' section explicitly flags 5 ambiguities: (1) No quantitative performance targets, (2) No relevance ranking criteria defined, (3) No filter specification, (4) No performance baseline or regression criteria, (5) 'Better UI' excluded from scope. All are directly derived from the vague feature description in feature-ambiguous.md.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: Impact map ambiguity #5 states: '"Better UI" excluded from scope. The non-MVP requirement "Better UI — Make it look nicer" cannot be planned: no design mockups or Figma links are provided, and no frontend repository is listed in the Repository Registry. This requirement is excluded from the implementation plan entirely.' No task covers UI changes.

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 task files follow the template structure with sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify/Create, Implementation Notes, Reuse Candidates, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Optional sections like API Changes (task 3), Dependencies (tasks 2, 5), and Verification Commands (tasks 1, 3, 4) are included where applicable and omitted otherwise, consistent with the template rule 'Omit sections that do not apply.'

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks contain a bold 'Assumptions pending clarification:' block within their Description section: task-1 has 2 assumptions (columns to index, performance measurement), task-2 has 3 (ranking algorithm, cross-entity search, language config), task-3 has 3 (filter dimensions, AND composition, query parameters), task-4 has 3 (latency target, cache TTL, load targets), task-5 has 2 (text search config, backward compatibility contract).

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks begin with '## Repository' followed by the single value 'trustify-backend' on the next line. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with detailed content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), and '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified in task-1-add-search-indexes.md, task-2-full-text-search.md, task-3-add-search-filters.md, task-4-search-performance-optimization.md, and task-5-search-integration-tests.md.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: All modified files exist in repo-backend.md: migration/src/lib.rs, entity/src/sbom.rs, entity/src/advisory.rs, entity/src/package.rs, modules/search/src/service/mod.rs, modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs, common/src/db/query.rs, tests/api/search.rs. Created files follow existing structural patterns: migration/src/m0002_search_indexes/mod.rs (follows m0001_initial pattern), modules/search/src/model/mod.rs (follows other modules' model/mod.rs pattern). No invented paths.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 references migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs pattern and entity files. Task 2 references modules/search/src/service/mod.rs, common/src/model/paginated.rs (PaginatedResults<T>), common/src/db/query.rs, common/src/error.rs (Result<T, AppError> with .context()). Task 3 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs pattern, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/list.rs (Axum Query<> extractor), common/src/db/query.rs. Task 4 references common/src/db/limiter.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs cache pattern. Task 5 references tests/api/search.rs, tests/api/sbom.rs, tests/api/advisory.rs, assert_eq!(resp.status(), StatusCode::OK) pattern.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks have '## Target Branch' followed by 'main': task-1 line 4, task-2 line 4, task-3 line 4, task-4 line 4, task-5 line 4.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 tasks end with '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-hex>': task-1: sha256-md:1b3f2569da3318a8c276664b22a7f6b1ef7560a83b03083c469724d786257140 (64 chars verified), task-2: sha256-md:2fa1e4901ac3298496076cc56085d940afa4549495a86878cc3cd2669bd801f3 (64 chars), task-3: sha256-md:9f43d7496e8d79d7a91c450c40063803024798446462aaf8048f4c8826afb9d6 (64 chars), task-4: sha256-md:56774af638d78159b7c0b5722c859dc928d1d1658a3f82d8d79fd3796d01b869 (64 chars), task-5: sha256-md:fa2d7af1e7fba7a63cc3aa86ad33f076fc6700f0f90b67fda12d440ff302d8fa (64 chars). All are exactly 64 lowercase hex characters, not placeholders or abbreviated values.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No task file contains a convention enrichment section or any 'Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>' rationale. The repo-backend.md manifest shows a CONVENTIONS.md file exists in the repository, so convention-aware enrichment should have been performed. While there are no 'Not applicable' annotations (which is correct), there is also no evidence of convention validation or inclusion with the prescribed rationale format in any of the 5 task files. The convention-applicability-rules.md requires applicable conventions to be included with rationale — the complete absence of any convention section means the enrichment step was not performed.
